steps.title: Identify the transformation rules
steps.content:
Function transformations follow these rules:
  • Translate right by $h$: replace $x$ with $(x - h)$
  • Translate down by $k$: subtract $k$ from the function
We need to go right 9 and down 3.

steps.title: Apply both transformations
steps.content: Start with $f(x)=\frac{a-18}{x}+6$. Replacing $x$ with $x-9$ moves the graph right $9$ units: $$f(x-9)=\frac{a-18}{x-9}+6$$ Then subtract $3$ to move the graph down: $$g(x)=\frac{a-18}{x-9}+6-3=\frac{a-18}{x-9}+3$$

steps.title: Check the choices with a sample constant
steps.content: Use a convenient value such as $a=24$ to graph the choices in Desmos. Then $a-18=6$ and $a-21=3$. The correct graph should be the original representative function shifted right $9$ and down $3$. Only choice B does that.
